compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: SUPERSEDED by / DUPLICATE of story 4cdf-e52c-e68e-4f7d ('Make the three settled verify defaults always-on (remove off switches)', CLOSED). The flag removal this ticket describes landed on main as commit 808643185 'config-prune: fossillike-blockish-warbler: make three settled verify defaults always-on' (rebar-ticket: 4cdf-e52c-e68e-4f7d; reachable from HEAD). Evidence: remediation_mode and novelty_drop_active are gone from src/rebar/_config_schema.py (only a historical comment at line 279 remains: 'switch (novelty_drop_active) was retired in story 4cdf'); no config-key consumers remain in plan_review/__init__.py, code_review/workflow_ops.py, or workflow/gate_dispatch.py; the rising floor is unconditional; docs/config.md + docs/plan-review-gate.md no longer document the keys. AC1-AC3 satisfied by 4cdf. AC4 (operator-attested soak) was the PRECONDITION for the removal, which has already shipped under 4cdf (and the default-ON flip 70c8 is closed) — so it is moot for this now-superseded ticket. Not self-attesting an operator soak sign-off; closing as superseded.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The cross-round convergence floors (plan-review rising floor + code-review region-gated floor) are being flipped to default-ON (ticket 70c8-423e-e4c1-4d86) by operator authorization. The flags (verify.remediation_mode, verify.novelty_drop_active) then exist only as a back-out. Once default-on has soaked in the field, the flags are dead configuration surface: remove them and make the floors always-on, per the operator directive.

## What
- Delete the `remediation_mode` and `novelty_drop_active` keys from src/rebar/_config_schema.py (fields + coercion entries) and remove every gate check that consults them (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py, src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py, src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py) so the floor path is unconditional (per-review eligibility in plan_review/attest.py remains the only gate).
- Remove/adjust the off-path tests (tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py, test_plan_review_convergence.py, test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py, test_code_review_region_gate.py) that exercise flag-off behavior; keep eligibility-gate tests.
- Update docs/config.md + docs/plan-review-gate.md (drop the keys from the tables; describe eligibility as the only gate). Note the unknown-key warning path will flag stale configs that still set them — acceptable per deprecation policy.
- Precondition: a field soak of default-ON with no must-suppress regressions (e.g. one week of dogfood rounds, no gold-set suppression incidents).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The two config keys are removed from the schema and all consuming gate checks; the rising floor runs whenever per-review eligibility holds, with no config switch.
- [ ] Off-path flag tests are removed/replaced; eligibility-path tests remain green; make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
- [ ] docs/config.md and docs/plan-review-gate.md no longer document the keys and describe eligibility as the sole gate.
- [ ] [operator-attested] a soak period with the floors default-ON has elapsed with no must-suppress (gold-set) regression reported; the attestation names the soak window and evidence.
compiled_state.title: Remove the convergence-floor flags: make remediation-mode rising floor always-on
